title: Adversarial Review System
context: Governance systems in this repo repeatedly pass CI and human review while failing to actually enforce the behaviors they claim to enforce. The root cause: the same system that writes the spec also writes the tests, and tests designed to confirm compliance will find it. The only review that breaks this cycle is adversarial: assume claims are false, derive what must necessarily exist if they were true, and check for that. This ADR introduces a two-phase CI check: a fast deterministic validator (Phase 1) and a stateful Letta Code adversarial agent review (Phase 2).

claims.statement: validate:pr fails when prs/<branch>.json is missing, malformed, or references non-existent ADR claims
claims.required_evidence: Running validate:pr against a branch with no PR description exits non-zero. Running it against a valid PR description exits zero.

claims.statement: The adversarial-review.yml workflow triggers on PR open and push to main, runs Phase 1 (deterministic) before Phase 2 (LLM), and blocks merge on FAIL verdict
claims.required_evidence: Workflow file exists with pull_request trigger, validate-pr-description job gates adversarial-review job via needs:, and adversarial-review job exits 1 on FAIL verdict

claims.statement: The Letta adversarial agent reads the PR description and referenced ADR JSON, adversarially checks each claim against codebase evidence, and produces a machine-parseable ADVERSARIAL_VERDICT line
claims.required_evidence: A real run of the adversarial agent against a PR with a hollow claim must produce ADVERSARIAL_VERDICT with verdict FAIL and a specific finding. Human attestation from the repo owner is required.

claims.statement: CODEOWNERS requires @glassbead-tc review for all governance artifacts: .adr/, .specs/, .schemas/, .github/workflows/, automation-self-improvement/control-plane/manifest.yaml
claims.required_evidence: .github/CODEOWNERS contains entries for all listed paths pointing to @glassbead-tc

claims.statement: The JSON schemas for ADR and PR description enforce required fields and enum values via Zod in the validator
claims.required_evidence: validate:pr returns schema-violation errors when required fields are missing or enum values are invalid

hypotheses.prediction: The adversarial agent will catch hollow test claims that deterministic CI does not
hypotheses.falsification: Run the adversarial agent against a PR that claims integration tests exist but only has mocks. If it returns PASS, the hypothesis fails.

hypotheses.prediction: The stateful memory of the adversarial agent improves catch rate over time as it accumulates codebase-specific failure patterns
hypotheses.falsification: After 10 PR reviews, the agent should catch at least one pattern it missed on the first run. If the catch rate does not improve, the hypothesis fails.

hypotheses.prediction: Requiring PR description JSON before LLM review eliminates the class of governance bypass where agents declare work done without structured evidence
hypotheses.falsification: If an agent can get a PR to CI green without a valid prs/<branch>.json, the hypothesis fails.

decisions.choice: Non-Anthropic, non-OpenAI model via Letta Code + OpenRouter
decisions.rationale: Adversarial reviewer must not exhibit in-family preference bias toward Claude-generated output

decisions.choice: Stateful Letta agent with persistent memory across all PR reviews
decisions.rationale: Memory of past hollow claims and codebase-specific failure patterns improves catch rate over time

decisions.choice: Two phases: deterministic fast check gates LLM review
decisions.rationale: Fail fast on structural issues before spending LLM credits. Deterministic check catches missing files and broken references; LLM review catches semantic hollowness.

decisions.choice: JSON in prs/<branch>.json, validated by Zod schema
decisions.rationale: Machine-readable, version-controlled, linter-enforceable, directly comparable to ADR JSON claims

consequences: Every PR to main must include prs/<branch>.json — no exceptions
New ADRs are JSON format; existing markdown ADRs are not migrated
Requires LETTA_API_KEY and ADVERSARIAL_REVIEWER_AGENT_ID in repo secrets
Adversarial agent must be created in Letta ADE with non-Anthropic/OpenAI model before CI can pass Phase 2
Behavioral claims in ADRs require agentic_test log or human attestation — unit tests are insufficient
